Project.NAME: japan-travel-rag-mcp
Project.keywords: japan-travel, mcp, fastmcp, oauth-proxy, logto, user-portal, landing, onboarding, root-auth, zod, contract-first
Project.annotation: Portal-first architecture keeps current four MCP proxy tools and adds shareable landing + self-serve user portal onboarding before deferred ingestion work.

M-CONFIG.NAME: Config
M-CONFIG.purpose: Runtime config validation for MCP OAuth proxy settings, admin auth, portal session/identity settings, and M2M provisioning credentials.

fn-loadConfig.description: Validate env and build typed AppConfig.

type-AppConfig.description: App config for tg-chat-rag, admin token, portal session, public URL, Logto credentials, and M2M provisioning credentials with configurable role ID.

M-LOGGER.NAME: Logger
M-LOGGER.purpose: Structured logger factory used by runtime, route handlers, and integration modules.

fn-createLogger.description: Create scoped logger with semantic block labels.

M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS.NAME: ToolContracts
M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS.purpose: Tool schema registry and validators for current proxied MCP tool set.

schema-SearchMessagesInputPublic.description: Public schema for search_messages with filters.chat_ids forbidden.

const-TOOL_INPUT_JSON_SCHEMAS.description: Tool descriptions and parameter contracts consumed by runtime registration.

M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT.NAME: TgChatRagClient
M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT.purpose: HTTP methods adapter for tg-chat-rag upstream API.

type-UpstreamCallError.description: Normalized upstream error surface for protocol/timeout/http failures.

M-AUTH-CONSENT-PATCH.NAME: ConsentPatch
M-AUTH-CONSENT-PATCH.purpose: Replace FastMCP built-in consent screen with portal-styled HTML via monkey-patching OAuthProxy.consentManager.

fn-generatePortalConsentScreen.description: Render portal-styled consent HTML with scope labels, XSS escaping, and form POST to /oauth/consent.

fn-formatScopeLabel.description: Map OAuth scope strings to human-readable labels.

M-AUTH-PROXY.NAME: FastMcpOauthProxy
M-AUTH-PROXY.purpose: FastMCP OAuthProxy integration for /mcp authentication backed by Logto OIDC upstream endpoints.

fn-createOauthProxy.description: Create OAuthProxy, apply portal consent patch, and provide authorization-server metadata for FastMCP oauth config.

type-OauthProxyDeps.description: Validated dependency bundle for proxy construction.

M-PORTAL-IDENTITY.NAME: PortalIdentityClient
M-PORTAL-IDENTITY.purpose: Identity-provider adapter for portal social OAuth start/callback flows and automatic tester access role assignment.

fn-buildPortalOauthStartUrl.description: Build provider-specific Logto authorization URL for social direct sign-in.

fn-handlePortalOauthCallback.description: Exchange callback code and resolve tester identity profile.

fn-ensureMcpAccessRole.description: Assign/verify MCP user role via dedicated M2M app credentials and configurable role ID without manual admin actions.

M-TOOL-PROXY.NAME: ToolProxyService
M-TOOL-PROXY.purpose: Tool execution orchestration: validate input, apply policy, call upstream, normalize MCP result.

fn-executeTool.description: Main proxy execution method for all supported tools.

fn-buildUpstreamPayloadWithPolicy.description: Inject internal chat scope for search_messages.

fn-authenticateAdmin.description: Validate login token and issue signed session cookie.

fn-requireAdminSession.description: Allow/deny decision for protected admin routes.

fn-clearAdminSession.description: Generate cookie invalidation header for logout.

M-PORTAL-AUTH.NAME: PortalAuth
M-PORTAL-AUTH.purpose: Portal tester session lifecycle management and route guards for /portal protected routes.

fn-authenticatePortalUser.description: Issue portal session after successful identity auth.

fn-requirePortalSession.description: Allow or redirect for protected portal routes.

fn-clearPortalSession.description: Invalidate tester portal session cookie on logout.

M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING.NAME: PortalProvisioning
M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING.purpose: Automatic post-registration/login provisioning for tester MCP access rights.

fn-provisionTesterAccess.description: Ensure required MCP tester role/policy exists for user.

M-ADMIN-UI.NAME: AdminUiRoutes
M-ADMIN-UI.purpose: Server-rendered admin login and ops routes guarded by AdminAuth.

M-PORTAL-UI.NAME: PortalUiRoutes
M-PORTAL-UI.purpose: Server-rendered landing + portal per-page routes for external tester onboarding with progressive enhancement.

fn-handleLandingRequest.description: Serve / landing with redirect action to portal.

fn-handlePortalRootRoute.description: Handle /portal and route to login/home by portal session state.

fn-handlePortalRegisterRoute.description: Handle /portal/register social-provider page without password fields.

fn-handlePortalLoginRoute.description: Handle /portal/login social-provider page without password fields.

fn-handlePortalOauthStartRoute.description: Handle /portal/auth/start and redirect to Logto social OAuth endpoint.

fn-handlePortalOauthCallbackRoute.description: Handle /portal/auth/callback and create portal session after provisioning.

fn-renderPortalLayout.description: Render shared portal page shell and include compiled Tailwind stylesheet asset.

fn-renderPortalConnectionGuide.description: Render MCP setup instructions for external AI agents.

M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.NAME: FastMcpRuntime
M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.purpose: Construct FastMCP runtime, register current tool surface, configure OAuth Proxy on /mcp, and mount admin + portal routes.
M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.depends: M-AUTH-PROXY, M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS, M-ADMIN-UI, M-PORTAL-UI

fn-createFastMcpRuntime.description: Build FastMCP instance with oauth proxy and route extension.

fn-registerProxyTools.description: Register current four tool handlers with Zod schemas.

fn-mountAdminRoutes.description: Mount /admin routes through Hono app returned by getApp().

fn-mountPortalRoutes.description: Mount /, /portal, and /portal/* routes through Hono app returned by getApp().

M-SERVER.NAME: ServerMain
M-SERVER.purpose: Bootstrap config and dependencies, then start FastMCP HTTP stream runtime with MCP, admin, and portal surfaces.
M-SERVER.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-AUTH-PROXY, M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-ADMIN-UI, M-PORTAL-IDENTITY, M-PORTAL-AUTH, M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING, M-PORTAL-UI, M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME

fn-main.description: Initialize runtime dependencies, mount route handlers, and start server lifecycle.

M-ROOT-ENTRYPOINT.NAME: RootEntrypoint
M-ROOT-ENTRYPOINT.purpose: Top-level process bootstrap and fatal error mapping.

fn-bootstrap.description: Invoke main() and terminate process on fatal startup error.

M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER.NAME: McpAuthProvider
M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER.purpose: Legacy mcp-auth provider integration scheduled for removal after OAuth Proxy migration.

fn-initMcpAuth.description: Legacy provider init kept only during transition.

M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER.NAME: McpAuthAdapter
M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER.purpose: Legacy authenticate(request) bridge scheduled for removal after OAuth Proxy migration.

fn-authenticateFastMcpRequest.description: Legacy request auth adapter for mcp-auth flow.

M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY.NAME: ApiKeyRepository
M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY.purpose: Legacy API-key repository outside simplified runtime target.

M-DB.NAME: DrizzleDb
M-DB.purpose: Legacy PostgreSQL bootstrap used by previously planned API-key lifecycle.

fn-createDb.description: Legacy DB bootstrap retained pending cleanup.

DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.NAME: portal onboarding flow
DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.step-1: User opens `/portal/register` or `/portal/login` and chooses social provider.
DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.step-2: `/portal/auth/start` redirects to Logto social OAuth flow and returns to `/portal/auth/callback`.
DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.step-3: M-PORTAL-IDENTITY resolves identity, M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING ensures MCP role, and M-PORTAL-AUTH issues session.
DF-PORTAL-ONBOARDING.step-4: M-PORTAL-UI renders MCP setup guide after successful callback flow.

DF-PORTAL-UI-INTERACTION.NAME: portal progressive enhancement flow
DF-PORTAL-UI-INTERACTION.step-1: Portal page is rendered on server using Bun JSX templates.
DF-PORTAL-UI-INTERACTION.step-2: HTMX updates social-provider selection/error fragments and can trigger OAuth start route requests.
DF-PORTAL-UI-INTERACTION.step-3: Alpine.js handles optional local UI state only (for example toggles/copy helpers).

DF-MCP-OAUTH.NAME: mcp oauth proxy flow
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-1: Client starts OAuth flow through FastMCP endpoints on `/mcp`.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-2: M-AUTH-CONSENT-PATCH renders portal-styled consent screen for user approval.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-3: After consent, M-AUTH-PROXY redirects to Logto upstream for authentication.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-4: M-AUTH-PROXY exchanges code/tokens with Logto and issues proxy session.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-5: Authenticated session can execute registered MCP tools.

DF-TOOL-PROXY.NAME: tool proxy flow
DF-TOOL-PROXY.step-3: M-TOOL-PROXY applies policy and calls M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT.
DF-TOOL-PROXY.step-4: Normalized result is returned via MCP transport.

DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.NAME: admin root auth flow
DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.step-2: M-ADMIN-AUTH verifies `ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN` and issues session cookie.
